title: 10 best herbal creams for acid reflux

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with acid reflux because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, glycyrrhizin forms glycyrrhetic acid, which coats and protects the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid. Additionally, licorice creams help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and providing relief from heartburn symptoms. As a result, herbal licorice creams offer a natural remedy for managing acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus from damage caused by acidic stomach contents.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in herbal licorice creams, have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe stomach ulcers associated with acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: Contains flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to calm digestive issues caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder measuring 2 grams per serving.
Mix the powder with distilled water at room temperature to create a paste.
Combine the paste with cold-pressed sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove from heat allow cooling to set before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The cream's active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, work to calm down the production of stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of it flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort. Additionally, the cream's warming sensation helps to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing food to pass through the digestive system more efficiently and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Digestive enzymes inhibitors: The phenolic compounds in ginger cream inhibit the production of digestive enzymes that break down food, allowing for a slower digestion process and reducing the risk of acidic stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus.
Antacid properties: Ginger's antacid properties help neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, providing quick relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, making them effective in reducing symptoms of acid reflux.

Roots: Ginger roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural antacid and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for creating creams that alleviate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root and rinse it thoroughly under running water.
Peel the ginger using a vegetable peeler and cut it into small pieces.
Combine the ginger pieces with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil and whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime creams helps with acid reflux because they possess a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's alkaline nature helps to neutralize stomach acidity, reducing inflammation in the esophageal lining and soothing the discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, herbal broad-leaved lime creams contain flavonoids that strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, thus providing effective relief from heartburn and indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lime herb contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Lime herb can neutralize free radicals that may contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the esophagus, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.

Gastric-soothing properties: Lime's bitter compounds may help stimulate digestive secretions, reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining and esophagus, thereby alleviating heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, which help to neutralize acidity and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the esophagus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Combine one tablespoon of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves with one cup of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix three tablespoons of beeswax and six tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Gradually add the herbal infusion to the beeswax-coconut oil mixture while whisking until well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with acid reflux because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically, chamomile cream can calm irritated tissues in the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties help eliminate harmful bacteria that can exacerbate symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, chamomile creams provide natural relief from acid reflux discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy improved digestion and a better quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile cream helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and discomfort.

Soothing property: The anti-irritant compounds in chamomile cream calm and soothe the mucous membranes lining the esophagus, reducing irritation caused by stomach acid and alleviating symptoms like burning and pain.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, preventing it from relaxing too much and allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, thus reducing frequency of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of acid reflux.

Flowers: The delicate flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease digestion, making them a popular choice for acid reflux relief.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50 grams of high-quality beeswax pellets and place them in a double boiler setup.
Add 150 milliliters of distilled water to the double boiler and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Combine 30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 30 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves in a small saucepan.
Infuse the herb mixture in 200 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Gradually add the cooled herbal infusion to the melted beeswax mixture, stirring until well combined and smooth, with a final consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with acid reflux because they contain natural oils that soothe the esophageal muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties reduce irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds, such as menthol and methanol, help relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms and pressure that can contribute to acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids present in peppermint cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and discomfort.

Sothing and coating property: Peppermint's soothing and coating properties can help protect and calm the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, providing a barrier against stomach acids and reducing irritation caused by acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves measuring approximately 20 grams for optimal potency.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 70 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over medium heat stirring occasionally for 20 minutes or until melted.
Strain the cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool at room temperature before use within 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with acid reflux because they provide a protective barrier in the esophagus, soothing and calming irritated mucous membranes. The natural mucilages in marshmallows create a gel-like substance that coats and cushions the lining of the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, marshmallow creams can help neutralize stomach acids and reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes, providing relief for individuals suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow creams contain a high concentration of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and coat the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and saponins present in marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and chest pain.
Antacid effects: Marshmallow creams contain a natural antacid that helps to neutralize stomach acid, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and providing rapid relief from symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20g of althaea officinalis flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and texture.
Combine dried flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse and release their active compounds into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix the reserved liquid with 200g of beeswax and 50ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl, stirring until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with acid reflux because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herb fennel contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the stomach more easily. This reduces symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and bloating. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce irritation in the esophagus and alleviate discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to relieve gas, bloating, and discomfort associated with acid reflux by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in fennel cream reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms such as heartburn and chest pain.
Antacid property: Fennel's antacid properties help neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve symptoms of acid reflux, including regurgitation of food and burning sensations in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural antiacid properties that can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or air dry.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture under low heat for 2 hours and then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with the infused oil and stir until melted, cooling to room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander creams helps with acid reflux because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the digestive tract. The herb coriander has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, coriander's antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, preventing excessive contractions that can worsen acid reflux symptoms. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Coriander's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in coriander cream, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the digestive tract, making it more resistant to acid reflux.

Carminative and anti-spasmodic properties: Coriander's essential oils have a calming effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, relaxing the esophageal sphincter and reducing contractions that can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the coriander plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming digestive issues associated with acid reflux.
Seeds: Coriander seeds have been traditionally used to treat indigestion and heartburn due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coriandrum sativum leaves and chop them into small pieces to release essential oils.
Combine 100g of chopped coriandrum sativum leaves with 200ml of coconut oil in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to infuse with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and heat gently until melted, then pour into jars for solidification at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with acid reflux because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel inside the aloe leaf contains antioxidants and amino acids that calm down the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining. This helps to neutralize acidity and alleviate heartburn symptoms. remedy_properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory agents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and caffeic acid, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.

Soothing mucilages: Aloe vera's gel contains mucilages, like acemannan, that form a protective barrier on the skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from acid reflux by coating and calming irritated tissues.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: Aloe rhyzomes contain soothing agents that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Gather essential herbs including aloe vera gel, ginger extract, and licorice root powder in equal proportions weighing 20 grams each.
Combine the dried herbs into a clean glass jar and infuse them in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 48 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, and store it in an airtight container for up to two months.
Mix the herbal oil with aloe vera gel in a ratio of 1:2, adding 5 grams of beeswax and heating the mixture over low heat until it reaches 120 degrees fahrenheit, stirring occasionally.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized jars, allowing them to cool and thicken at room temperature before storing in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with acid reflux because they possess a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural oils and extracts from the damask rose plant, such as beta-carotene and vitamin A, help to calm the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Damask rose contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating acid reflux symptoms.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: The emollient and soothing properties of damask rose extracts help to soften and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

Antioxidant properties: Damask rose's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which are associated with acid reflux development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing acid reflux symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the damask rose plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosa damascena flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of the ground flowers with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring constantly for 30 minutes.
Combine 10g of sweet almond oil and 10g of coconut oil in a separate saucepan and heat it gently until warm, then slowly add 2g of vitamin e oil.
Gradually pour the oil mixture into the beeswax-flower mixture while stirring continuously until well combined and smooth, taking about 10 minutes.
Pour the resulting cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon creams helps with acid reflux because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The soothing properties of ingredients like slippery elm and marshmallow root help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, while the antacid properties of ingredients like calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide help to neutralize stomach acid and relieve heartburn symptoms. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal lemon creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like ginger, turmeric, or slippery elm, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate acid reflux symptoms by soothing the mucous membranes.

Gastric soothers: Herbal lemon creams may contain soothing agents like marshmallow root, licorice root, or slippery elm, which help calm the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate heartburn symptoms by providing a protective barrier against gastric acids.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 250ml of extra virgin olive oil, 50g of dried lemon balm leaves.
Combine ingredients by mixing the olive oil and lemon balm leaves in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Add 10ml of freshly squeezed lemon juice and mix well for another 5 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with acid reflux because of its unique properties that provide relief from heartburn and digestive discomfort. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in cornflowers help to reduce inflammation and soothe the esophageal mucosa, reducing irritation caused by stomach acid. Additionally, the creams' natural emollient properties create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, helping to neutralize acid and prevent it from splashing back up into the esophagus, providing long-lasting relief from acid reflux symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centaurea cyanus flowers and dry them in a warm, dark place for at least 24 hours.
Crush 20 grams of dried centaurea cyanus flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 100 grams of beeswax pellets, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 25 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for approximately 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until all ingredients are fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify completely before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with acid reflux because it contains natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. The herbal extract calms irritation and reduces inflammation, which can occur due to excessive stomach acid production or weak lower esophageal sphincter muscles. Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb borage (Borago officinalis) contains anti-inflammatory compounds like boragine and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and ease symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and bloating.

Soothing glycosides: Borage cream contains soothing glycosides like boroegenin, which help to calm the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, these parts help reduce inflammation and acidity in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried borago officinalis flowers and measure out 20 grams for the infusion.
Combine the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a glass jar and steep for 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax pellets with 60 milliliters of coconut oil and heat gently over low heat until melted.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring well to incorporate before pouring into sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with acid reflux because they contain the potent anti-inflammatory compound, echinacea. This natural ingredient soothes and calms the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, the creams' antioxidant properties help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort in the chest and throat. Gastroprotective effects: Kansas coneflower has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which means it can help protect the stomach lining from acid damage and reduce the risk of ulcers, a common complication of acid reflux.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root powder and 20 grams of beeswax.
Infuse the powder in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for eight hours.
Mix 50 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter with the infused oil blend.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes to melt the beeswax and emulsify the ingredients.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before pouring into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with acid reflux because of its unique combination of natural ingredients that provide rapid relief from heartburn and indigestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried vitis vinifera leaves from organic sources in early summer months for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested leaves with 20 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a warm dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth and filter out any remaining plant particles before combining with beeswax and emulsifiers.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with acid reflux because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems in early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or low-temperature oven method for 1-2 hours.
Blend dried verbena officinalis powder with 20% beeswax and 30% coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under gentle stirring at 150°f to 160°f for 15-20 minutes until beeswax melts and mixture thickens.
Fill sterilized containers with the cooled cream and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves by cutting them during full moon.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation method for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried st john's wort with three parts sweet almond oil in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into small glass jars and store them in a cool dark place.

The anti-inflammatory properties of cucumber help to neutralize stomach acid, while its antioxidant content protects the esophagus from further damage. Additionally, cucumber's cooling effect provides immediate relief from burning sensations and discomfort in the chest and throat, providing a natural and gentle solution for managing acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal cucumber creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cucurbitacins, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Sothing and moisturizing property: Cucumber's high water content and emollient properties can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Antacid and neutralizing property: Certain herbs like licorice root and marshmallow root used in herbal cucumber creams have been shown to possess antacid properties, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce acidity that can exacerbate acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, cucumber roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe acid reflux symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like cucurbitacins and cucumin, rhyzomes from the cucumber plant are used to reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cucumis sativus leaves and stems from your garden or local health food store.
Dry the cucumis sativus plant material using a dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried cucumis sativus with three parts coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized jars and store at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with acid reflux because they contain bioactive compounds that soothe and protect the digestive tract. The bitter principles in dandelion, such as taraxasterol, stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties help to heal and seal the lining of the esophagus, preventing acid from flowing back up into the throat and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, help soothe and reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, reducing symptoms of acid reflux.

Digestive aid: The bitter compounds in dandelion, like sesquiterpene lactones, stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to alleviate heartburn and indigestion associated with acid reflux.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content helps protect the esophageal mucosa from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems, rinse thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the harvested plants with 500ml of distilled water in a saucepan, simmer gently for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, pressing on solids to extract liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the extracted plant liquid, heat in a double boiler until melted.
Pour the hot herbal mixture into sterilized jars, allow to cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for acid reflux are topical preparations that utilize natural ingredients to soothe and calm the esophageal mucosa, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. These creams contain herbs such as aloe vera, slippery elm, and marshmallow root, which create a protective barrier against stomach acids. For example, creams containing licorice extract have been shown to significantly reduce symptoms of acid reflux in patients. By providing instant relief from burning sensations and discomfort, herbal creams for acid reflux improve daily life by allowing individuals to enjoy their favorite foods without worrying about post-meal distress.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acid reflux with herbal creams is to look for formulas containing natural anti-inflammatory agents such as aloe vera, marshmallow root, and slippery elm. These ingredients help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Regular application of these creams can provide fast and effective relief from heartburn symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acid reflux that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and skin irritation. These conditions often share symptoms such as itching, burning, and redness, which can be alleviated by topical application of herbal creams. Herbs like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the skin and provide relief from discomfort.
